function.name: supertool
function.description:
(1) Reads, writes and searches local files (operation = list, read, write, append); (2) downloads URLs to files (operation = download); (3) runs python3 or bash scripts in a sandboxed environment (operation = run).
All operations share a file space. All file paths shoudl be relative, including in scripts run with the 'run' command.
Read operations may be capped to a maximum length as noted in return values; start_index and read_length can be used to read incrementally. Similarly, write oeprations may be size-limited; larger files can be built up using the append command.
For the run command, both standard output and error streams are returned. Installed python3 packages are: annotated-types,attrs,beautifulsoup4,certifi,charset-normalizer,click,contourpy,cycler,et_xmlfile,fonttools,idna,joblib,jsonschema,jsonschema-specifications,kiwisolver,lightgbm,lxml,matplotlib,more-itertools,mpmath,narwhals,nltk,numpy,nvidia-nccl-cu12,openpyxl,packaging,pandas,patsy,pillow,plotly,pyarrow,pydantic,pydantic_core,pyparsing,python-dateutil,pytz,PyYAML,RapidFuzz,referencing,regex,requests,rpds-py,scikit-learn,scipy,seaborn,six,soupsieve,statsmodels,sympy,tabulate,threadpoolctl,toml,tqdm,typing-inspection,typing_extensions,urllib3,xgboost,xlrd. Unix tools available are: grep,sed,awk,sort,uniq,cut,tr,wc,head,tail,find,diff,cat,jq,sqlite3.

operation.description: Action to perform: 'list' returns files in 'path' sorted most recently modified at the top. All other operationss work on a single file. 'read' returns file contents. 'grep' runs grep -E to search for a regular expression. 'write' writes a file. 'append' adds content to the end of a file. 'download' saves the contents of a URL to a file. 'run' executes a python3 or bash script.

path.description: The relative path on which to perform operation (required for all operations except 'run').

contents.description: For 'write' and 'append' only, text content to write.

max_files.description: For 'list' only, optional max number of files to return. Not present or 0 implies all.

start_index.description: For 'read' only, the character position to start reading from. Not present implies 0.

read_length.description: For 'read' only, the maximum characters to read. Not present or 0 implies all (subject to configured max).

extract_text.description: For 'download' only, false returns original content (usually html). true extracts only readable text. Not present implies true.

regex.description: For 'grep' only, the extended regular expression.

case_insensitive.description: For 'grep' only, true uses a case-insensitive search. Not present implies false.

cmd.description: For 'run' only, the command line to execute (e.g., 'ls' or 'python3 myscript.py')
